questions: how does diplomacy work between countries?
answers: Diplomacy is the practice of managing international relations through negotiation, dialogue, and peaceful communication between nations rather than conflict.
Diplomats represent their countries abroad, negotiate treaties, protect citizens overseas, and build relationships with foreign governments to advance national interests.
Diplomacy kaje lage karon eta countries er moddhe peace maintain kore, trade agreements hoy, ar conflicts resolve hoy without war.
Modern diplomacy involves embassies, international summits, trade negotiations, and multilateral agreements that shape global politics and cooperation between nations.
